name: Restart-Computer
-ComputerName
-CN
-__SERVER
-Server
-IPAddress
options.description: Specifies one computer name or a comma-separated array of computer names. `Restart-Computer` accepts ComputerName objects from the pipeline or variables.

options.name: -Credential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to do this action. The default is the current user.

options.name: -Delay
options.description: Specifies the frequency of queries, in seconds. PowerShell queries the service specified by the For parameter to determine whether the service is available after the computer is restarted.

options.name: -For
options.description: Specifies the behavior of PowerShell as it waits for the specified service or feature to become available after the computer restarts. This parameter is only valid with the Wait parameter.

name: -Force
-f
options.description: Forces an immediate restart of the computer.

name: -Timeout
-TimeoutSec
options.description: Specifies the duration of the wait, in seconds. When the timeout elapses, `Restart-Computer` returns to the command prompt, even if the computers aren't restarted.

options.name: -Wait
options.description: `Restart-Computer` suppresses the PowerShell prompt and blocks the pipeline until the computers have restarted. You can use this parameter in a script to restart computers and then continue to process when the restart is finished.

options.name: -WsmanAuthentication
options.description: Specifies the mechanism that is used to authenticate the user credentials. This parameter was introduced in Windows PowerShell 3.0.

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running `Restart-Computer`.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if the `Restart-Computer` runs. The `Restart-Computer` cmdlet isn't run.

args.name: -ComputerName
args.description: Specifies one computer name or a comma-separated array of computer names. `Restart-Computer` accepts ComputerName objects from the pipeline or variables.

args.name: -Credential
args.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to do this action. The default is the current user.

description: Restarts the operating system on local and remote computers.
